prompt: Paper-collage stop-motion assembly. Use Image 1 as the exact empty first frame (a flat burnt-orange paper field) and Image 2 as the exact completed last frame. One continuous locked-off vertical shot, no camera movement. Open on the empty burnt-orange paper field, then assemble the scene piece by piece with crisp physical stop-motion timing, PACED EVENLY ACROSS THE ENTIRE CLIP: pieces keep sliding in and snapping into place continuously through the whole shot — do NOT finish early, do NOT hold a long static frame; the rows of filing cabinets slide in and stack on the right and their drawers open filled with plain document folders, then a robotic hand made of circuit-board fragments slides in from the left and reaches into an open drawer, and in the final second it pulls a folder out while a burst of paper and data fragments scatters upward.
